title: EQT to acquire Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring Exolaunch, a rideshare launch services provider for satellites, to address increasing launch demand.

title: Space Force’s rapid acquisition office director moves to Air Force Nuclear Weapons Center
summary: Kelly Hammett is transitioning from her role as director of the Space Force’s rapid acquisition office to the Air Force Nuclear Weapons Center. This move coincides with the Space Rapid Capabilities Office being realigned under the Space Force.

title: Chinese startup Spark Space tests engine, raises funds for electric-pump rocket
summary: Chinese startup Spark Space has conducted engine tests and secured funding for what it claims will be the world's largest electric-pump-fed rocket. The company aims to advance its rocket technology through these developments.

title: A legacy to help solve the space workforce pipeline
summary: The Challenger Center's president and CEO, Mike Kincaid, discusses the space workforce pipeline and the center's role in an episode of Space Minds. The conversation highlights efforts to address workforce challenges in the space industry.

title: What the satellite servicing economy can borrow from carbon credits
summary: The FCC authorized 15,000 Starlink Gen2 satellites in January 2026, while Starcloud and SpaceX have filed for 88,000 and 1 million orbital data center satellites, respectively. The article discusses how the satellite servicing economy could adopt principles from carbon credits.

title: Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated a quantum networking protocol in ground testing for a payload scheduled for a 2027 launch. The experiment involved 'high-fidelity entanglement swapping' as part of an on-orbit experiment.

title: Swift reboost mission ready for launch
summary: A high-risk mission to raise the orbit of NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft is scheduled to launch later this month after less than a year of development. The mission aims to extend the spacecraft's operational lifespan.

title: Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad
summary: Blue Origin has initiated the rebuilding of a launch pad that was severely damaged in a recent New Glenn rocket explosion. The company aims to resume launches by the end of the year.

title: Quantum Space wins Pentagon contract to develop orbital refueling spacecraft
summary: Quantum Space has secured a Pentagon contract to develop an orbital refueling spacecraft, aiming to deliver the fuel-transfer vehicle to the U.S. Space Force by 2028.

title: Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission
summary: Relativity Space announced plans to privately develop and launch a Mars orbiter mission by 2028. This initiative marks the company's expansion into planetary exploration beyond Earth orbit.

title: Scientists trace high-energy ghost particle to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy
summary: Scientists have traced a high-energy ghost particle, a neutrino, to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy. If confirmed, this would be the first direct link between an individual dusty star-forming galaxy and a high-energy neutrino event.

title: James Webb Space Telescope finds a salty surprise on famous 'Pink Planet'
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered that the exoplanet known as the 'Pink Planet' contains salt. This finding marks a significant observation in the study of cold exoplanets.

title: Watch SpaceX launch new batch of US spy satellites from California early on June 19
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch a new batch of US spy satellites from California on June 19, with the event available for live viewing.

title: You can get my favorite image-stabilized binoculars for their cheapest ever price in this incredible anti-Prime Day deal
summary: The Fujinon Techno-Stabi TS-L 1640 image-stabilized binoculars are on sale for $1,125 at Walmart, offering 16x magnification and 40mm objective lenses for stargazing.

title: Sun-like star may have swallowed an exoplanet with help from a mysterious companion: 'You are what you eat, right?'
summary: A Sun-like star may have consumed an exoplanet, possibly influenced by an unseen companion. This discovery highlights the dynamic and mysterious nature of stellar evolution.

title: Astrophotographer captures a cosmic lagoon glowing 5,200 light-years from Earth (photo)
summary: An astrophotographer captured an image of the Lagoon Nebula, located 5,200 light-years from Earth. The nebula is best observed in the Northern Hemisphere around August.

title: These Mars valleys are hiding 30 dust devils | Space photo of the day for June 18, 2026
summary: Europe's Mars Express orbiter captured an image of a vast valley system on Mars, revealing the presence of 30 dust devils across the landscape. The discovery highlights dynamic atmospheric activity on the Red Planet.

title: 'It's quite a bit more than we expected': Satellite reveals immense scale of GPS signal tampering
summary: An experimental satellite has mapped the scale of GPS jamming across Europe and the Middle East from space, revealing a much larger problem than anticipated.

title: Astronomers solve the mystery of black holes' delayed cosmic 'burps'
summary: A new study explains why black holes emit delayed radio 'burps' years after consuming stars, providing a chemical blueprint to predict these events early.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is surveying distinct rock bands with varying textures and tones as it ascends Mount Sharp. The mission involves analyzing geological layers to understand Mars' past environment.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study space weather impacts on Earth's atmosphere and improve prediction capabilities for technology like GPS and satellites. The mission will advance to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has awarded contracts to eight new companies and expanded agreements with six existing ones to acquire commercial satellite data for research and decision-making. The new data will complement NASA's Earth satellites by providing high-resolution and frequent observations.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shares his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA. His story highlights resilience and the pursuit of space exploration despite humble beginnings.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested a prototype four-wheel rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert in March 2026. The rover was used to evaluate advanced mobility and robotic autonomy capabilities.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing a prototype rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy and navigation for future Moon and Mars missions. The rover recently traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that asteroid Donaldjohanson has a wobbly, peanut-like shape and a complex history, formed from fragments after a violent collision 155 million years ago.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Clouds. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs in a cold gas and dust filament beyond the Orion Nebula.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright in X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. Researchers found that the cluster is actually two clusters merging along the line of sight.

title: El Niño Is Underway
summary: Satellite observations confirm that El Niño conditions are intensifying in June 2026, as indicated by rising sea surface heights. This natural climate phenomenon is expected to strengthen further in the coming months.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 robotic arm as the CRS-34 cargo mission departs. The repairs are part of ongoing maintenance to ensure the arm's functionality for future operations.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with the second stage recently transported to Stennis Space Center for testing. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace aborted the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider faced weeks of postponements before scrubbing the mission.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This marks a key step toward the agency's goal of returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams have begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm technology that is being highlighted as a significant advancement in the space industry. This innovation is expected to have broad applications across various space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to improve training, operations, and simulations, while also engaging the public with space exploration. This initiative opens new pathways for space exploration through immersive technologies.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test, likely related to a landing system, is undergoing a critical bake-out process to ensure its reliability. This step is crucial for preparing the parachute for deployment in a future mission.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite, paving the way for a planned 2027 flight. The test marks a significant milestone in quantum communication technology.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned. The mission marks a significant milestone for the company's autonomous space capabilities.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans its first GEO mission in 2027, following a near-Earth mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace, a New Zealand-Dutch space transportation company, raised $25 million in a Series B funding round. The funds will be used to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data centers for defense purposes.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera secured £3 million in funding to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into space applications.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a pullback in other publicly traded space companies, causing major players to decline. Investors reacted by reducing holdings in competing space firms following SpaceX's strong market debut.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking the first successful flight of its new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security applications.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration aims to enhance Norway's disaster response capabilities using advanced satellite technology.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described it as a 'defining milestone' for the firm.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a newly formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite capabilities. The acquisition follows Lyntris's recent formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund is investing $26 million in the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite project. This investment is part of a larger coalition supporting the non-profit Earth-based initiative.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the space industry. It also mentions some breaking news related to the conversation.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying it to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following recent U.S. Space developments.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to enhance protection against cyber threats in the space sector.
